description: Analyze the PAM recognition mechanism of the Streptococcus pyogenes Cas9 complex using PDB:4UN3. You must identify the four polymer chains (Cas9 protein, sgRNA, target DNA, non-target DNA), locate the two highly conserved PAM-interacting Arginine residues (Arg 1333 and Arg 1335 in Cas9), and identify the corresponding Guanine nucleotides on the non-target DNA strand that they hydrogen bond with. Measure the two specific hydrogen-bond distances (in Angstroms) between the Arginine sidechain nitrogens and the Guanine Hoogsteen edge atoms. Produce a publication-quality figure highlighting this interface and save it to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/cas9_pam_recognition.png. Also, write a structural report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/cas9_pam_report.txt. The report must contain: the PDB ID (4UN3), the chain assignments (which chain letter corresponds to Cas9, sgRNA, Target DNA, and Non-target DNA), the residue numbers of the two PAM-interacting Arginines (1333 and 1335), the residue numbers of their target Guanines on the non-target strand, and the two distance measurements in Angstroms.
